items.title: Arcane Jaspr
items.content: Arcane Jaspr Neon Arcane Jaspr is the Flutter-first surface for Jaspr. The primary path is , with HTML wrappers and raw Jaspr moved behind secondary imports. Start Here - $1 - $1 - $1 - $1 - $1 What Changed - The primary import now exposes , , , , , and - HTML-first wrappers such as , , and now live on - Raw Jaspr escape hatches now live on - The component catalog is generated from the current export surface instead of hand-maintained counts Canonical Reference Use the $1 as the current source

items.title: Deployment
items.content: Deployment Deploy your static documentation site to various hosting providers. Firebase Hosting The template includes Firebase Hosting configuration. Setup 1. Install Firebase CLI: 2. Login to Firebase: 3. Initialize (already done in template): Deploy Build and deploy in one command: Or use the script: Other Providers Since the output is static HTML, you can deploy anywhere: Vercel Netlify GitHub Pages 1. Build the site 2. Copy contents to your branch 3. Enable GitHub Pages in repository setting

items.title: Design Tokens
items.description: Reference for all Arcane UI design tokens
items.content: Design Tokens Arcane Jaspr uses a comprehensive design token system for consistent styling. These tokens are available as both Dart constants and CSS custom properties. Colors (ArcaneColors) All colors are available as CSS variables with the prefix. Brand & Accent Colors | Token | CSS Variable | Description | |-------|--------------|-------------| | | | Primary accent color | | | | Accent hover state | | | | Accent container background | | | | Text on accent background | Status Colors | Token |

items.title: Import Surfaces
items.description: There is no alias layer; use the primary import first and advanced imports only by choice
items.content: Import Surfaces Arcane Jaspr does not ship a short-name alias layer. There is no , and the primary documentation path does not assume one. Primary Import Use this for normal app code. It exposes the Flutter-shaped base types and the Arcane widget surface. Advanced Imports Use only for low-level HTML wrapper components such as , , and . Use only when you intentionally want raw Jaspr and DOM APIs. Naming Rule The default authoring path keeps the explicit names. That keeps package examples, generat

items.title: Installation
items.description: Build the canonical counter app with the Flutter-first Arcane Jaspr surface
items.content: Quick Start This is the baseline Arcane Jaspr example. If you can write this app, you are on the intended primary surface. Counter App What This Confirms - You imported only - You used , , , and - You did not need explicit type arguments in normal widget usage - You did not need HTML wrappers or raw Jaspr types Next Steps - Browse $1 - Read $1 - Read $1

items.title: Introduction
items.description: Arcane Jaspr primary surface and documentation map
items.content: Introduction Arcane Jaspr gives Jaspr a Flutter-shaped app surface without forcing you back into raw HTML or raw Jaspr APIs. Primary Import This is the normal app path. It includes the Flutter-shaped base types, Arcane widgets, renderers, stylesheets, and theming utilities. Advanced Imports Use only when you intentionally want low-level HTML wrappers. Use only when you intentionally want raw Jaspr or DOM access. Same UI in Plain Jaspr Plain Jaspr is still a strong fit when you want direct DOM co

items.title: Quick Start
items.description: Add Arcane Jaspr to a Jaspr project or scaffold a project with Oracular
items.content: Installation There are two supported starting points: scaffold a project with Oracular or add Arcane Jaspr to an existing Jaspr app. Option 1: Scaffold with Oracular The repo-owned templates already target the Flutter-first Arcane Jaspr surface. Option 2: Add Arcane Jaspr to an Existing Jaspr App Prerequisites - Dart SDK - Jaspr CLI Add the Package Use the Primary Import Only add these if you intentionally need them: Wrap the App Root Run the App Build Static Output

items.title: Styling
items.description: Learn how to style components using ArcaneStyleData
items.content: Styling Arcane Jaspr uses for declarative, type-safe styling. This approach provides autocomplete, compile-time checking, and consistent design tokens. ArcaneStyleData The class is the foundation of all styling in Arcane Jaspr. It converts typed properties into CSS. Basic Usage Using Raw CSS For properties not covered by typed enums, use the parameter: Static Presets ArcaneStyleData provides static presets for common layouts: Row Layout Row with Space Between Column Layout Centered Content Full

items.title: Theming
items.description: Learn how to configure stylesheets and theming in Arcane Jaspr
items.content: Theming Arcane Jaspr uses a stylesheet-based theming system built on CSS custom properties. Stylesheets provide complete design systems including colors, typography, spacing, and component renderers. ArcaneApp Every Arcane application must be wrapped in an component which provides the theme context and injects the stylesheet CSS. Available Stylesheets Arcane Jaspr includes two stylesheets: | Stylesheet | Description | |------------|-------------| | | Minimal, modern, accessible. Based on shadcn/

items.title: Why Jaspr?
items.description: When to choose Jaspr over Flutter for web development
items.content: Why Jaspr + Arcane Jaspr? This guide helps you understand when Jaspr is the right choice for your web project, especially compared to Flutter web. The Core Difference $1 renders your UI to a canvas element. The browser sees a single tag, not your buttons, text, or links. $1 renders to semantic HTML. Your button is a , your text is a , your link is an . The browser understands your content. This fundamental difference affects everything. --- SEO and Discoverability Search Engine Indexing | Aspect
